key_insight: The paper gives black box algorithms to recognize Suzuki groups Sz(q), construct standard generators, test membership, and produce straight-line programs without needing an explicit matrix representation.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical group-theoretic computations in computational algebra systems when only oracle access to the group is available.
method: Construct standard generators for Sz(q) using a one-sided Monte Carlo algorithm.
Perform constructive membership testing and express elements as straight-line programs in the standard generators.
Provide an efficient presentation of Sz(q) that can be verified algorithmically.
evidence_signal: The abstract states the algorithms are implemented in Magma and are one-sided Monte Carlo with no false positives.
watch_item: Because the recognition procedure is Monte Carlo, it may require probabilistic runs and careful handling of rare failure cases or runtime variability.
paper_title: Black Box Recognition of the Suzuki groups
